sourceMovie.title: John Q
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: John Q (2002) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Nick Cassavetes's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Negotiator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If John Q clicked for you, The Negotiator (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after John Q?
faq.answer: Try The Negotiator, Layer Cake, A Perfect World—each shares DNA with John Q while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is John Q?
faq.answer: John Q (2002) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is John Q worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
